insight: Tunisia's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows that the team was able to gain momentum in half of the breaks they had, with a slight overall decline in momentum. When Tunisia was pressing before a hydration break, they tended to lose momentum, with an average decline of 0.200 points per minute, and were unable to regain it after the break. However, when they were not pressing before a break, they were able to slightly increase their momentum, with an average gain of 0.100 points per minute. This suggests that Tunisia may benefit from adjusting their strategy before hydration breaks, potentially by easing off on the press to conserve energy and come out stronger after the break.
